title: C++ Type Casting
content:
### Type Casting in C++

Type casting converts a value from one data type to another. C++ provides several casting mechanisms.

---

### C-Style Cast

```cpp
int a = 10;
double b = (double)a;          // C-style cast
double c = double(a);          // Function-style cast
```

---

### C++ Cast Operators

| Cast | Purpose | Syntax |
|------|---------|--------|
| `static_cast` | Standard conversions | `static_cast<type>(expr)` |
| `dynamic_cast` | Polymorphic class casting | `dynamic_cast<type*>(ptr)` |
| `const_cast` | Remove const/volatile | `const_cast<type>(expr)` |
| `reinterpret_cast` | Bit pattern reinterpretation | `reinterpret_cast<type>(expr)` |

---

### static_cast

```cpp
double pi = 3.14159;
int approx = static_cast<int>(pi);  // 3 (truncates)

char ch = static_cast<char>(65);    // 'A'
void* ptr = ≈
int* intPtr = static_cast<int*>(ptr);
```

---

### dynamic_cast

Used for safe downcasting in class hierarchies:

```cpp
Base* basePtr = new Derived();
Derived* derivedPtr = dynamic_cast<Derived*>(basePtr);
if (derivedPtr) {
  // Safe to use derivedPtr
}
```

---

### const_cast

```cpp
const int value = 5;
int* modifiable = const_cast<int*>(&value);
```

---

> **Key Point:** Prefer C++ casts (`static_cast`, `dynamic_cast`, `const_cast`, `reinterpret_cast`) over C-style casts. They are safer, more explicit, and checked by the compiler.

quiz.question: Which C++ cast is used for standard type conversions?
